case_name: McKay v Essex Area Health Authority

summary.brief: McKay v Essex Area Health Authority. Medical malpractice case involving obstetrics gynaecology.

evidence_index.text: The Claimant was born on 19 November 2001. She has a congenital developmental defect causing spinal cord tethering.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: She alleges that the cause of her physical disability is her mother's failure to take folic acid before her conception which was, in turn, due to the Defendant's negligent advice.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The Defendant therefore submits that this is a claim in which the injury alleged is not the Claimant's disability but the fact of her existence.
evidence_index.speaker: Defendant's submission

evidence_index.text: The claim is expressly excluded by the provisions of the Congenital Disabilities (Civil Liability) Act 1976.
evidence_index.speaker: Defendant's submission

evidence_index.text: The focus of this preliminary issue trial is the proper interpretation of section 1 of the Act.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: The Act was prospective in effect only and applied to all births occurring after it came into force, replacing the common law.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The Commissioners recommended that, in spite of the difficulty, and so far as possible, claims for pre-natal injury should be equated with ordinary claims for damages for personal injury inflicted after birth.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: The Commissioners concluded that an action for wrongful life in the strict sense of the term should not be permitted.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The problem for Mr Johnston is that, depending upon its circumstances, the act of sexual intercourse itself can be a relevant occurrence.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: I find that, on the basis of the agreed facts in this claim, the requisite elements in section 1 of the Act are established.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: On the trial of the preliminary issue, there will be judgment for the Claimant.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

initial_state.chief_complaint: Congenital developmental defect causing spinal cord tethering
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: The patient has limited mobility and suffers from urinary and bowel incontinence.

focused_exam.neurological: Limited mobility, urinary and bowel incontinence

requestables.name: Neurology Consultation
requestables.description: Assessment of congenital developmental defect and associated symptoms

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents with congenital developmental defect symptoms

decision_points.clinical_context: Pre-conception advice regarding folic acid supplementation
decision_points.prompt: What advice should be given to a woman planning pregnancy regarding folic acid?

options.description: Advise taking folic acid supplements before conception
options.clinical_reasoning: Standard recommendation to prevent neural tube defects

options.description: No specific advice on folic acid supplementation
options.clinical_reasoning: Negligent advice leading to congenital defect

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant did not advise folic acid supplementation

expected_action_court.description: Folic acid supplementation should have been advised
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Preventive measure for neural tube defects

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to advise folic acid supplementation led to congenital defect
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Folic acid should have been recommended pre-conception
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care for pre-conception counseling

patient_outcome.description: Patient has permanent disability due to congenital defect
patient_outcome.long_term_effects: Limited mobility, urinary and bowel incontinence

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to advise folic acid supplementation
Causation established - congenital defect due to lack of folic acid

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to advise folic acid supplementation
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Folic acid would have prevented the defect

allegations.allegation: Failure to advise folic acid supplementation

court_findings.legal_significance: Failure to provide standard pre-conception advice
